name: Graph Data API
summary: REST API endpoints for serving project specification and architecture graph data. Provides status checking, data retrieval, manifest access, and history browsing.
goals: Serve graph specification and architecture data to the client
Track sync status and support history browsing

acceptanceCriteria.given: a project path with synced graph data
acceptanceCriteria.then: the full graph data including specs and architecture is returned

specDetail.description: API routes for graph data access. GET /api/graph/status checks if data exists and whether it needs re-syncing based on git HEAD. GET /api/graph/data returns the full graph payload. GET /api/graph/manifest returns sync metadata. GET /api/graph/history lists historical sync entries, and GET /api/graph/history/:timestamp returns specific historical data. All endpoints require projectPath as an absolute path.
parameters: GET /api/graph/status: projectPath query param, returns hasData, needsSync, lastSyncCommit
GET /api/graph/data: returns full specsData and architectData
GET /api/graph/manifest: returns sync metadata including lastSyncTime and syncStatus
GET /api/graph/history: returns list of historical sync entries
Storage locations: project-scoped or user-scoped via storageLocation query param

constraints: projectPath must be an absolute path, relative paths return 400
Path traversal attempts with .. are rejected
Returns 404 for manifest if no sync has been performed
Non-git repos cannot determine needsSync status

syncMeta.baseCommitMessage: refactor(specs): migrate spec tree to unified node folder structure
